artPrompt: An evocative scene of a folding-chair meeting room where a long table is ringed by far too many empty chairs and only a sparse handful of seated townsfolk, an unseen rule hanging in the air as a soft golden glow tilts the whole room toward emptiness. Dust motes drift, a gavel rests beside a forgotten casserole, the absurd stalled vote made visible. Leafy green and ivory light, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: The Quorum That Isn't
text:
A vote at this table now requires a quorum: it counts only if the number of people physically in the room is greater than the number of empty chairs. Otherwise the motion is tabled until your next snack.
While in effect, whenever a vote is tabled, you create a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token, because procedure produces volunteers the way puddles produce mosquitoes.

extras.fourthWall: counts people and empty chairs in the room
